entry.title: Bill 59 (Hawaiʻi County): Adopted → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 24, ARTICLE 9, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PEDESTRIANS

Prohibits certain acts by pedestrians as follows: “Receiving or soliciting rides for employment or business unless legally parked; if convicted of a crime within one year of a previous violation; standing, watching or guarding of a vehicle while parked on a street or highway.”

Status: Adopted → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-07-09

View on the council site

entry.title: New: Bill 59 (Hawaiʻi County) — Adopted
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 24, ARTICLE 9, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PEDESTRIANS

Prohibits certain acts by pedestrians as follows: “Receiving or soliciting rides for employment or business unless legally parked; if convicted of a crime within one year of a previous violation; standing, watching or guarding of a vehicle while parked on a street or highway.”

Latest action: Council: Bill 59, Draft 2 passed second & final reading - 7/9/2025

View on the council site
